description: Accounts, dated October 1230 CE, in the hand of Shelomo b. Eliyyahu, referring to a cellar (maṭmūra) in his father’s house with 117 jugs of wine. Most of the wines are described as ‘crossbred’ (muwallad), and just 10 are described as ‘real wine’ (khamr). (Information from CUDL.) See also DK 201.2.
tags: Shelomo b. Eliyyahu, 13th c, wine, account, Partnership
description_he: רשימה או טבלה. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
